reasoning: Magnus Carlsen is currently ranked #1 in the FIDE classical ratings with an Elo of 2839 as of November 1, 2025. This gives him a massive 26-point lead over the next highest-rated player, Hikaru Nakamura. Due to the Elo system mechanics and the K-factor for top players, closing this rating gap would require an exceptional performance from Nakamura or an unusually poor string of performances from Carlsen. While there is a slight amount of uncertainty as the forecast horizon extends to early 2026 (allowing for potential rating shifts in major upcoming classical tournaments like Tata Steel Chess), Carlsen's dominance and the sheer margin of his lead make it extremely likely he will retain his #1 ranking through all three resolution dates. There is no evidence of inactivity risking his removal from the list, nor of any ongoing tournament expected to cost him his lead by mid-to-late November or early December 2025. Given Wikipedia's typical update schedule, the November 16 date is exceptionally safe as it will reflect the already-published November FIDE list. The subsequent dates hold only marginally higher risk.
